WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
prerequisite: Strong foundation in mathematics, algorithmic problem-solving, and proficient programming skills
course_objectives: Develop a strong understanding of algorithm design techniques for efficient problem-solving.
Analyze algorithm complexity and explore NP-completeness, approximation, and randomized algorithms.
course_outcomes: CO1: Analyse algorithm efficiency using recurrence relations and sorting techniques.
CO2: Solve optimization problems with Greedy algorithms (MST, Shortest Path).
CO3: Implement Dynamic Programming for Knapsack, Matrix Chain Multiplication, etc.
CO4: Apply Backtracking and Branch & Bound to combinatorial problems.
CO5: Understand NP-Completeness, Approximation, and Randomized algorithms.

units.title: Introduction & Sorting in Linear Time
topics: Recurrence Relations: substitution, Master's method, Change of Variables
Linear time sorting: Counting sort, Radix sort, Bucket sort

units.title: Greedy Algorithms
topics: Task scheduling with deadline and penalty problem
Minimum Spanning trees: Kruskal's algorithm, Prim's algorithm
Single source shortest paths: Dijkstra's and Bellman Ford algorithms

units.title: Dynamic Programming
topics: All pair shortest paths: Warshal's and Floyd's algorithms

units.title: Backtracking & Branch and Bound
topics: Hamiltonian Cycles and Sum of subsets
Branch and Bound: Traveling Salesman Problem

units.title: String Matching, Approximation Algorithm
topics: Approximation algorithms: TSP, Hamiltonian problem, Vertex Cover Problem
Introduction to NP-Completeness: Class P and NP
Polynomial reduction, NP Completeness Problem, NP-Hard Problems

textbooks: Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, and Stein - Introduction to Algorithms (3rd ed.), MIT Press, 2009. ISBN 978-0262033848
reference_books: Dasgupta, Papadimitrou and Vazirani - Algorithms (3rd ed.), McGraw-Hill Education, 2006. ISBN 978-0073523408
Design and Analysis of Computer Algorithms by Aho, Hopcroft and Ullman, Pearson
Horowitz, Sahni, and Rajasekaran - Computer Algorithms (2nd ed.), Silicon Press, 2007. ISBN 978-0929306414
Kleinberg and Tardos - Algorithm Design (1st ed.), Pearson, 2005. ISBN 978-0321295354
